title: podman build running from within a systemd notify service generates erroneous systemd image
body:
### Issue Description

When running podman build from within a systemd `Type=notify` service (e.g. a Jenkins build job), podman will include a `/run/systemd/notify` directory in the generated image. If the generated image CMD is systemd, systemd will fail to boot the image because `/run/systemd/notify` already exists.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

The following dockerfile generates a simple and working systemd image.
```dockerfile
FROM ubuntu:23.04
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y systemd
CMD ["/lib/systemd/systemd"]
```
Generating the image from within a systemd "notify" unit (e.g. a Jenkins build job) will generate the Image as well:
```
[Unit]
Description=build erroneous podman systemd image

[Service]
User=user
Type=notify
TimeoutSec=infinity
ExecStart=/bin/sh -c "/usr/bin/podman build /home/user/tests && /usr/bin/systemd-notify --ready"

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```
But it additionally contains a `/run/systemd/notify` directory, which will cause problems later on.

### Describe the results you received

Running the generated image of the notify service using `podman run` will fail.
```
systemd 252.4-1ubuntu1 running in system mode (+PAM +AUDIT +SELINUX +APPARMOR +IMA +SMACK +SECCOMP +GCRYPT -GNUTLS +OPENSSL +ACL +BLKID +CURL +ELFUTILS +FIDO2 +IDN2 -IDN +IPTC +KMOD +LIBCRYPTSETUP +LIBFDISK +PCRE2 -PWQUALITY +P11KIT +QRENCODE +TPM2 +BZIP2 +LZ4 +XZ +ZLIB +ZSTD -BPF_FRAMEWORK -XKBCOMMON +UTMP +SYSVINIT default-hierarchy=unified)
Detected virtualization podman.
Detected architecture x86-64.

Welcome to Ubuntu Lunar Lobster (development branch)!

bind(/run/systemd/notify) failed: Address already in use
[!!!!!!] Failed to start up manager.
Exiting PID 1..
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expected the generated image not to contain the `/run/systemd/notify` directory.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

running podman build from within a systemd type=notify service

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Looks to me that systemd must create that directory during its install.  Podman is just going to create an image matching whatever content was created.

If  you don't want content in the container image, then remove it with a run command

RUN rm -rf /run/systemd/notify

Nothing Podman can do for this situation.

You could probably run the generated container with

podman run --tmpfs /run:notmpcopyup ...
